heading: Senior leaders of the Department of Defense and other specified persons: authority to provide protection

Temporary Extension of Authority To Provide Security for Former Department of Defense Officials, , , provided authority to provide security for former Department of Defense Officials from , to .Pub. L. 117–263, div. A, title XI, § 1105136 Stat. 2817Dec. 23, 2022Dec. 23, 2022Jan. 1, 2024

children.text: Secretary of Defense.
Deputy Secretary of Defense.
Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff.
Vice Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff.
Secretaries of the military departments.
Members of the Joint Chiefs of Staff in addition to the Chairman and Vice Chairman.
Commanders of combatant commands.

children.heading: .—Protection for Former or Retired Department Leadership
children.label: b .—Protection for Former or Retired Department Leadership

children.text: previously served in a position identified in paragraphs (1) through (7) ; and1So in original. Probably should be followed by “of subsection (a)”.1
faces serious and credible threats arising from duties performed while employed by the Department of Defense.

children.label: A
children.text: there is a serious and credible threat to the safety of the individual for whom protection is to be provided; or

children.label: B
children.text: compelling operational considerations make such protection essential to the conduct of official Department of Defense business.

children.label: A
children.text: Any official or employee of the Department of Defense or member of the armed forces.

children.label: B
children.text: A former or retired official who faces serious and credible threats arising from duties performed while employed by the Department.

children.label: C
children.text: A head of a foreign state, an official representative of a foreign government, or any other distinguished foreign visitor to the United States who is primarily conducting official business with the Department of Defense.

children.label: D
children.text: Any member of the immediate family of a person authorized to receive physical protection and personal security under this section.

children.label: E
children.text: An individual who has been designated by the President, and who has received the advice and consent of the Senate, to serve as Secretary of Defense, but who has not yet been appointed as Secretary of Defense.

children.heading: .—Limitation on delegation
children.label: 3 .—Limitation on delegation
children.text: The authority of the Secretary of Defense to authorize the provision of physical protection and personal security under this subsection may be delegated only to the Deputy Secretary of Defense or the Under Secretary of Defense for Intelligence and Security.

children.label: A
children.text: pending the determination of the Secretary under paragraph (4) regarding such individual; and

children.label: B
children.text: for a period not to exceed 30 days.

children.heading: .—Requirement for Written Determination
children.label: d .—Requirement for Written Determination
children.text: A determination of the Secretary of Defense whether to provide physical protection and personal security under subsection (b) or (c), or reimbursement under subsection (h), shall be in writing, shall be based on a threat assessment by an appropriate law enforcement, security, or intelligence organization, and shall include the name and title of the officer, employee, or other individual affected, the reason for such determination, the duration of any authorized protection and security for such officer, employee, or individual, and the nature of any arrangements for such protection and security.

children.heading: .—Duration of Protection
children.label: e .—Duration of Protection
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall require periodic reviews, not less than once every six months, of the duration of protection provided to individuals under subsection (b) or (c).

children.label: A
children.text: An initial determination made under subsection (d), not later than 15 days after the date on which the determination is made, including the justification for such determination and a current threat assessment by an appropriate law enforcement, security, or intelligence organization.

children.label: i
children.text: the justification for such determination;

children.label: ii
children.text: a current threat assessment by an appropriate law enforcement, security, or intelligence organization; and

children.label: iii
children.text: a certification that threats to the individual arising from duties performed while employed by the Department of Defense can be sufficiently mitigated without physical protection and security or reimbursement.

children.label: i
children.text: the justification for such determination;

children.label: ii
children.text: a current threat assessment by an appropriate law enforcement, security, or intelligence organization; and

children.label: iii
children.text: a certification that threats to the individual arising from duties performed while employed by the Department of Defense can be sufficiently mitigated without protection and security or reimbursement.

children.label: i
children.text: the justification for such determination;

children.label: ii
children.text: a current threat assessment by an appropriate law enforcement, security, or intelligence organization; and

children.label: iii
children.text: a certification that threats to the individual arising from duties performed while employed by the Department of Defense can be sufficiently mitigated without reimbursement.

children.heading: .—Form of report
children.label: 2 .—Form of report
children.text: A report submitted under paragraph (1) may be made in classified form.

children.heading: .—Regulations and guidelines
children.label: 3 .—Regulations and guidelines
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall submit to the congressional defense committees the regulations and guidelines prescribed pursuant to subsections (b) and (c)(1), and a description of any changes to such guidelines, not less than 20 days before the date on which such regulations take effect.

children.label: A
children.text: An individual described in subsection (c)(2)(C) who is otherwise sponsored by the Secretary of Defense, the Deputy Secretary of Defense, the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, or the Vice Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff.

children.label: B
children.text: An individual described in subsection (c)(2)(E).

children.heading: .—Notification to Certain Protected Personnel
children.label: g .—Notification to Certain Protected Personnel
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall provide written notification to any individual receiving physical protection and personal security under subsection (a) or (b), or reimbursement under subsection (j), at least 90 days prior to terminating or denying the renewal of protection and security protection or reimbursement, as the case may be, for such individual.
The Army Criminal Investigation Command.
The Naval Criminal Investigative Service.
The Air Force Office of Special Investigations.
The Defense Criminal Investigative Service.
The Pentagon Force Protection Agency.

children.heading: .—No additional law enforcement or arrest authority
children.label: 1 .—No additional law enforcement or arrest authority
children.text: Other than the authority to provide protection and security under this section, nothing in this section may be construed to bestow any additional law enforcement or arrest authority upon the qualified members of the armed forces and qualified civilian employees of the Department of Defense.

children.heading: .—Posse comitatus
children.label: 2 .—Posse comitatus
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to abridge .section 1385 of title 18

children.heading: .—Authorities of other departments
children.label: 3 .—Authorities of other departments
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to preclude or limit, in any way, the express or implied powers of the Secretary of Defense or other Department of Defense officials, or the duties and authorities of the Secretary of State, the Director of the United States Secret Service, the Director of the United States Marshals Service, or any other Federal law enforcement agency.

children.heading: .—Reimbursement
children.label: j .—Reimbursement
children.text: The Secretary of Defense may reimburse a former or retired official who faces serious and credible threats arising from duties performed while employed by the Department for security services and equipment procured at the personal expense of the official, not to exceed an aggregate of $15,000,000 in any fiscal year for all former and retired officials authorized by the Secretary of Defense for such reimbursement.
